title: Graph Main View
description: Studio UI for **Graph Main View**: the central workspace view for the Code Graph. It surfaces global graph statistics, index status, and provides a centralized view of high-risk architectural hotspots. Studio UI component: **Graph Main View**.
context: Studio UI for **Graph Main View**: the central workspace view for the Code Graph. It surfaces global graph statistics, index status, and provides a centralized view of high-risk architectural hotspots. Studio UI component: **Graph Main View**.

rules.requirement: view renders graph overview and index controls
rules:
When `GraphMainView` is active in the central workspace, it MUST display a high-level summary of the codebase's graph state.
The view MUST show the current index status (`getGraphStatus`), distinguishing between "Ready", "Stale", and "Off".
It MUST provide a manual "Reindex" button that triggers `indexGraph({ force: false })` and displays the progress/result in the global Output panel.
It MUST display aggregate counts for Specifications, Files & Docs (code files and documents), and Code Symbols sourced from the graph status.

The view MUST fetch and display architectural hotspots using the `getHotspots` port method.
It MUST render a list of the most critical symbols (e.g., top 10), showing their `riskLevel` as a Badge (e.g., `CRITICAL` in red), the symbol name and kind, direct/indirect dependencies, and the specs that currently cover them.
Components MUST consume data through `SpecdDataPort` hooks and MUST NOT import `@specd/core`.

rules.requirement: view surfaces loading and error states
rules: While requests are in flight or fail, the UI MUST show loading indicators and human-readable errors.

scenarios.requirement: view renders graph overview and index controls
scenarios.name: Graph status is rendered
then: AND it renders the "Ready", "Stale", or "Off" status
AND it renders counts for Specs, Files & Docs, and Symbols

scenarios.requirement: view renders graph overview and index controls
scenarios.name: Reindex triggers API and output log
then: AND it pushes an info message to the Output panel

scenarios.name: Hotspots are loaded and rendered
then: AND renders the symbols with their risk levels (e.g., CRITICAL) and dependency counts

scenarios.name: View uses hooks exclusively
when: UI package dependency graph is inspected
then: AND it does not import from @specd/core

scenarios.requirement: view surfaces loading and error states
scenarios.name: Loading state is shown for hotspots
then: a loading indicator is displayed in the Hotspots section
